rulings.Case Title: JOHN LAVAYSSE VS. THOMAS A KARABIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To (1) Compel The Deposition Of Michael Hall; And (2) For The Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 19, 2016, Line 1: PLAINTIFF JOHN LAVAYSSE'S Motion To (1) Compel The Deposition Of Michael Hall; And (2) For The Award Of Monetary Sanctions. OFF CALENDAR, Notice of Settlement of entire case filed. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY UNOBSKEY VS. SIMON MICHAEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion In Support Of Request For Preliminary Injunction Against Sidney Unobskey
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 19, 2016, Line 2: DEFENDANT SIMON MICHAEL'S Motion In Support Of Request For Preliminary Injunction Against Sidney Unobskey. GRANTED. Moving party sustained its burden. Opposing party failed to establish privilege. Plaintiff to produce the survey forthwith. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSALINDA CONSTANTINO VS. ONE WEST BANK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 19, 2016, Line 3: DEFENDANT CIT BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first cause of action for plaintiff to allege fraud with requisite specificity as to each element of cause of action for concealment, including the basis for moving defendant's liability as to the (1) concealment inducing Plaintiff to move in and (2) concealment inducing Plaintiff to stay. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Causes of action 2-7 are sufficiently alleged. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TIPTON MANAGEMENT COMPANY INC. A CALIFORNIA VS. RICHARD MCVICKER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 19, 2016, Line 4: PLAINTIFF TIPTON MANAGEMENT COMPANY INC.'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS. GRANTED as requested. The deposition costs incurred in a different action are not recoverable in this action. "Copies, postage & case file organization" costs are not recoverable. Defendant failed to show that costs incurred for hand delivery of motions and discovery was reasonably necessary and reasonable in amount. =(501/REQ)
